99: ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusType.byteLookup : ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusTypeByte lookup. Format `(op, a, b, c)`, where `op` selects the operation on bytes `b`, `c`: 0 `AND` (`a = b & c`), 1 `OR`, 2 `XOR`, 3 `U8Range` (`a = 0`), 4 `LTU` (`a = [b < c]`), 5 `MSB` (`a = b >> 7`, `c = 0`), 6 `Range` (`a < 2^b`, `c = 0`, `b ≤ 16`).
98: ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusType.pcLookup : ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusTypeProgram lookup: fetch the instruction at the current pc. Payload has 16 fields.
97: ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusType.memory : ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusTypeTo access a memory cell, instructions send the previous record and receive the new one. Payload: `(clk... (2 fields), address... (3 limbs), data... (4 × 16-bit limbs))`.
96: ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusType.executionBridge : ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusTypeThe CPU state ("execution bridge"): an instruction receives the current `(clk, pc)` and sends the next one. Payload: `(clk... (2 fields), pc... (3 fields))`.
95: ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusType : TypeThe SP1 bus types that appear in the default bus map.
94: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.defaultBusMap : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.BusMapThe hard-coded default OpenVM bus map, mirroring powdr's `default_openvm_bus_map`
92: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.openVmBusSemantics (p : ℕ) (busMap : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.BusMap := ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.defaultBusMap) : BusSemantics pThe OpenVM bus semantics for a given bus map (default: the hard-coded default bus map).
91: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.breaksInvariant {p : ℕ} (busMap : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.BusMap) (msg : BusInteraction (ZMod p)) : BoolWhether a message breaks an invariant on which soundness depends.
9: Variable : TypeA circuit variable: a display `name` plus an optional powdr witness-column ID.
89: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.BusMap : TypeA mapping from bus IDs to bus type.
87: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.violates {p : ℕ} (busMap : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.BusMap) (msg : BusInteraction (ZMod p)) : BoolWhether a message conflicts with the lookup table of the bus it is sent on.
86: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusType.tupleRangeChecker (size1 size2 : ℕ) : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusTypeCheck that a tuple of two values is in a certain range. Format: `(x, y)` where `x < size1` and `y < size2`.
85: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusType.bitwiseLookup : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusTypeCheck that a value is in a certain range. Format: `(x, y, z, op)` where `op` is either 0 (range check) or 1 (xor). The bus checks that `x` and `y` are bytes, and either `op = 0 ∧ z = 0` (range check) or `op = 1 ∧ z = x ^ y` (xor).
84: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusType.variableRangeChecker : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusTypeCheck that a value is in a certain range. Format: `(value, bits)`
83: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusType.pcLookup : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusTypeLookup to get the instruction flags from the current pc. Format: `(pc, flags...)`
79: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusType.memory : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusTypeTo access a memory cell, instructions should: 1. Receive a `(address_space, pointer, data... (4 bytes), prev_timestamp)` tuple 2. Send a `(address_space, pointer, data... (4 bytes), current_timestamp)` tuple.
76: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusType.executionBridge : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusTypeA instruction should receive a `(pc, timestamp)` and send updated values to the bus.
75: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.OpenVmBusType : TypeThe OpenVM bus types that appear in the default bus map.
74: MemoryBusShape.setNewMult {p : ℕ} (shape : MemoryBusShape) : ZMod pThe multiplicity a `setNew` carries on this bus (`1` for `receiveThenSend`, `-1` for `sendThenReceive`); the `getPrevious` reading it back carries the negation.
71: admissibleMemoryBus {p : ℕ} (shape : MemoryBusShape) (L : List (BusInteraction (ZMod p))) : PropGiven an ordered list of memory bus interaction messages *on the same bus*, decide whether it follows the memory bus discipline: after a `setNew` to a given address (multiplicity `shape.setNewMult`), the next `getPrevious` from the same address (multiplicity `-shape.setNewMult`) observes the same payload, with no intervening active messages to the same address.
70: MemoryBusDirectionWhich of the matched consecutive `setNew`/`getPrevious` pair is the send and which the receive (see `MemoryBusDirection`).
69: List ℕPayload positions forming the access key (e.g. OpenVM's two-limb address). Can be empty for a single global cell.
67: MemoryBusShape : TypeA description of a memory bus interaction.
66: MemoryBusDirection.sendThenReceive : MemoryBusDirection`getPrevious` sends (`1`), `setNew` receives (`-1`) — so `setNewMult = -1`. This is SP1's memory convention (it sends the previous record and receives the new one).
65: MemoryBusDirection.receiveThenSend : MemoryBusDirection`getPrevious` receives (`-1`), `setNew` sends (`1`) — so `setNewMult = 1`. This is OpenVM's memory convention (send the new record, receive the previous), and every VM's execution bridge, which sends the next CPU state and receives the current one.
63: MemoryBusDirection : TypeA memory access is a `getPrevious` (reading the cell's current value) followed by a `setNew` (committing its next value); the two carry opposite multiplicities. The variant is named for that order — `⟨getPrevious's operation⟩Then⟨setNew's operation⟩` — which fixes which is the *send* (multiplicity `1`) and which the *receive* (`-1`).
60: Optimizer.isCorrect {p : ℕ} (optimizer : Optimizer p) (busSemantics : BusSemantics p) (b : DegreeBound) : PropAn optimizer is correct if, for every input constraint system, replacing it with the optimized system is both sound and complete, and the optimizer respects the degree bound `b`.
6: ZMod : ℕ → TypeThe integers modulo `n : ℕ`.
59: Prod.{u, v} (α : Type u) (β : Type v) : Type (max u v)The product type, usually written `α × β`. Product types are also called pair or tuple types. Elements of this type are pairs in which the first element is an `α` and the second element is a `β`. Products nest to the right, so `(x, y, z) : α × β × γ` is equivalent to `(x, (y, z)) : α × (β × γ)`. Conventions for notations in identifiers: * The recommended spelling of `×` in identifiers is `Prod`.
57: optimizerRespectsDegreeBound {p : ℕ} (b : DegreeBound) (optimizer : ConstraintSystem p → ConstraintSystem p × Derivations p) : PropWhether an optimizer respects a degree bound: a within-bound input always yields a within-bound output.
56: DegreeBound : TypePer-zkVM bound on the multiplicative degree of a circuit's expressions.
54: ConstraintSystem.withinDegree {p : ℕ} (s : ConstraintSystem p) (b : DegreeBound) : PropWhether a constraint system stays within a degree bound.
53: ConstraintSystem.isCompleteReplacementOf {p : ℕ} (optimizedCS originalCS : ConstraintSystem p) (busSemantics : BusSemantics p) (ds : Derivations p) : PropWhether an optimized constraint system is a complete replacement for an original one. Assuming every input variable carries a powdr ID, then for any admissible satisfying assignment of the original constraint system, there is a computable assignment of the optimized system that is itself satisfying and admissible, with equivalent side effects.
51: Derivations.witgen {p : ℕ} (ds : Derivations p) (inputEnv : Variable → ZMod p) : Variable → ZMod pWitness generation: reconstruct an output assignment from an input assignment. Every powdr-ID (input) variable passes through unchanged; every other variable is computed by the method `ds` records for it, read from the input variables. This is what powdr runs to fill the optimized circuit's variables from an input trace.
50: Derivations (p : ℕ) : TypeA list of derived variables paired with how to compute each, in order — the extra output of the optimizer, consumed by witness generation.
48: ComputationMethod.ifEqZero {p : ℕ} (cond : Expression p) (thenM elseM : ComputationMethod p) : ComputationMethod p
47: ComputationMethod.quotientOrZero {p : ℕ} (num den : Expression p) : ComputationMethod p
45: cond.{u} {α : Sort u} (c : Bool) (x y : α) : αThe conditional function. `cond c x y` is the same as `if c then x else y`, but optimized for a Boolean condition rather than a decidable proposition. It can also be written using the notation `bif c then x else y`. Just like `ite`, `cond` is declared `@[macro_inline]`, which causes applications of `cond` to be unfolded. As a result, `x` and `y` are not evaluated at runtime until one of them is selected, and only the selected branch is evaluated.
44: ComputationMethod (p : ℕ) : TypeA method for computing a *derived* variable's value from other variables, mirroring powdr's `ComputationMethod`. For newly introduced variables, this is interpreted by powdr's witness generator. `quotientOrZero num den` is `num / den` in the field, or `0` when `den = 0`; `ifEqZero cond thenM elseM` picks `thenM` when `cond` evaluates to `0`, else `elseM`.
43: ConstraintSystem.isSoundReplacementOf {p : ℕ} (optimizedCS originalCS : ConstraintSystem p) (busSemantics : BusSemantics p) : PropWhether an optimized constraint system is a sound replacement for an original constraint system. Informally, for any satisfying assignment of the optimized system, there exists a corresponding satisfying assignment of the original system *with equivalent side effects*. Also, the optimized system must maintain all invariants guaranteed by the original system.
42: ConstraintSystem.admissible {p : ℕ} (s : ConstraintSystem p) (busSemantics : BusSemantics p) (env : Variable → ZMod p) : PropWhether a given assignment is admissible under the bus semantics.
41: ConstraintSystem.satisfies {p : ℕ} (s : ConstraintSystem p) (busSemantics : BusSemantics p) (env : Variable → ZMod p) : PropWhether a constraint system is satisfied under a given environment and bus semantics, i.e., whether it satisfies all algebraic constraints and does not violate any bus constraints.
38: The universe of propositions. `Prop ≡ Sort 0`. Every proposition is propositionally equal to either `True` or `False`.
37: List (BusInteraction (ZMod p)) → PropA property on *stateful* bus messages with nonzero multiplicity. Completeness is only required for assignments whose stateful messages are `admissible`. One useful way to use this is to describe the semantics of memory buses, see ``ApcOptimizer/MemoryBus.lean``.
36: BusInteraction (ZMod p) → BoolWhether sending this bus interaction message breaks an invariant on which soundness of the system depends. For example, a memory bus might have the invariant that all sent values must be in a certain range.
35: BusInteraction (ZMod p) → BoolWhether sending this bus interaction message violates a constraint in *another* chip. An example of this is sending a message that conflicts with a lookup table entry.
34: Bool : TypeThe Boolean values, `true` and `false`. Logically speaking, this is equivalent to `Prop` (the type of propositions). The distinction is public important for programming: both propositions and their proofs are erased in the code generator, while `Bool` corresponds to the Boolean type in most programming languages and carries precisely one bit of run-time information.
33: ℕ → BoolWhether the bus of the given ID changes the state of the VM. Stateless bus interactions are typically lookups.
32: BusSemantics.mk {p : ℕ} (isStateful : ℕ → Bool) (violatesConstraint breaksInvariant : BusInteraction (ZMod p) → Bool) (admissible : List (BusInteraction (ZMod p)) → Prop) : BusSemantics p
31: BusState (p : ℕ) : TypeThe effect on the stateful buses: the messages sent, each with a multiplicity.
30: BusSemantics (p : ℕ) : TypeThe bus semantics of the zkVM.
3: A type universe. `Type ≡ Type 0`, `Type u ≡ Sort (u + 1)`.
27: ConstraintSystem.sideEffects {p : ℕ} (cs : ConstraintSystem p) (busSemantics : BusSemantics p) (env : Variable → ZMod p) : BusState pThe side effects of a constraint system under a given environment and bus semantics. The side effects are the tuples sent to the *stateful* buses.
24: ConstraintSystem.mk {p : ℕ} (algebraicConstraints : List (Expression p)) (busInteractions : List (BusInteraction (Expression p))) : ConstraintSystem p
23: ConstraintSystem (p : ℕ) : TypeA constraint system representing a single zkVM chip.
22: List.{u} (α : Type u) : Type uLinked lists: ordered lists, in which each element has a reference to the next element. Most operations on linked lists take time proportional to the length of the list, because each element must be traversed to find the next element. `List α` is isomorphic to `Array α`, but they are useful for different things: * `List α` is easier for reasoning, and `Array α` is modeled as a wrapper around `List α`. * `List α` works well as a persistent data structure, when many copies of the tail are shared. When the value is not shared, `Array α` will have better performance because it can do destructive updates.
2: Nat : TypeThe natural numbers, starting at zero. This type is special-cased by both the kernel and the compiler, and overridden with an efficient implementation. Both use a fast arbitrary-precision arithmetic library (usually [GMP](https://gmplib.org/)); at runtime, `Nat` values that are sufficiently small are unboxed.
18: BusInteraction.mk {α : Type} (busId : ℕ) (multiplicity : α) (payload : List α) : BusInteraction α
16: BusInteraction (α : Type) : TypeA bus interaction. Typically, α is - an expression (=> symbolic bus interaction), or - a field element (=> bus interaction message).
15: Expression.degree {p : ℕ} : Expression p → ℕThe multiplicative degree of an expression.
13: Expression.eval {p : ℕ} (e : Expression p) (env : Variable → ZMod p) : ZMod pEvaluate an expression under an assignment `env` of variables to field elements.
12: Expression.mul {p : ℕ} (e1 e2 : Expression p) : Expression p
119: ApcOptimizer.SP1.koalaBear : ℕThe KoalaBear field modulus, `2^31 - 2^24 + 1` — the field all powdr SP1 exports use.
118: ApcOptimizer.SP1.sp1Optimizer (busMap : ApcOptimizer.SP1.BusMap := ApcOptimizer.SP1.defaultBusMap) (b : DegreeBound := ApcOptimizer.SP1.defaultDegreeBound) : ConstraintSystem ApcOptimizer.SP1.koalaBear → ConstraintSystem ApcOptimizer.SP1.koalaBear × Derivations ApcOptimizer.SP1.koalaBearOptimizer specialized for the SP1 semantics.
117: ApcOptimizer.SP1.sp1Optimizer_maintainsCorrectness (busMap : ApcOptimizer.SP1.BusMap) (b : DegreeBound) : Optimizer.isCorrect (ApcOptimizer.SP1.sp1Optimizer busMap b) (ApcOptimizer.SP1.sp1BusSemantics ApcOptimizer.SP1.koalaBear busMap) b
116: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.babyBear : ℕThe BabyBear field modulus, `2^31 - 2^27 + 1` — the field all powdr OpenVM exports use.
115: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.openVmOptimizer (busMap : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.BusMap := ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.defaultBusMap) (b : DegreeBound := ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.defaultDegreeBound) : ConstraintSystem ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.babyBear → ConstraintSystem ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.babyBear × Derivations ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.babyBearOptimizer specialized for the OpenVM semantics.
114: ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.openVmOptimizer_maintainsCorrectness (busMap : ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.BusMap) (b : DegreeBound) : Optimizer.isCorrect (ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.openVmOptimizer busMap b) (ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.openVmBusSemantics ApcOptimizer.OpenVM.babyBear busMap) b
113: simpleOptimizer {p : ℕ} (bs : BusSemantics p) (b : DegreeBound) : ConstraintSystem p → ConstraintSystem p × Derivations pOptimizer which does not use any bus facts. Works with any VM, but is less effective. Returns the optimized system together with the `Derivations` for its newly-introduced columns.
112: simpleOptimizer_maintainsCorrectness {p : ℕ} (bs : BusSemantics p) (b : DegreeBound) : Optimizer.isCorrect (simpleOptimizer bs b) bs b
111: optimizerWithBusFacts {p : ℕ} {bs : BusSemantics p} (b : DegreeBound) (facts : BusFacts p bs) (cs : ConstraintSystem p) : ConstraintSystem p × Derivations pThe fact-aware circuit optimizer: given proven `BusFacts` (which fixes the implicit `bs`), run the pipeline and return the output system with the `Derivations` for its new variables.
110: BusFacts (p : ℕ) (bs : BusSemantics p) : Type 1Proven, pass-consumable knowledge about the bus semantics `bs`.
108: optimizerWithBusFacts_maintainsCorrectness {p : ℕ} (bs : BusSemantics p) (b : DegreeBound) (facts : BusFacts p bs) : Optimizer.isCorrect (optimizerWithBusFacts b facts) bs b

105: ApcOptimizer.SP1.sp1BusSemantics (p : ℕ) (busMap : ApcOptimizer.SP1.BusMap := ApcOptimizer.SP1.defaultBusMap) : BusSemantics pThe SP1 bus semantics for a given bus map (default: the hard-coded default bus map).
104: ApcOptimizer.SP1.BusMap : TypeA mapping from bus IDs to bus type.
102: ApcOptimizer.SP1.violates {p : ℕ} (busMap : ApcOptimizer.SP1.BusMap) (msg : BusInteraction (ZMod p)) : BoolWhether a message conflicts with the lookup table of the bus it is sent on.
101: ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusType.pageProt : ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusTypePage-protection lookup. Payload has 6 fields.
100: ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusType.instructionFetch : ApcOptimizer.SP1.Sp1BusTypeInstruction-fetch lookup (the untrusted instruction table). Payload has 22 fields.
